findings.evidence: Lines 73-84 define `ComoduleCat.zero` as `abbrev ... PUnit` plus `zero_carrier`/`zero_coact`; lines 122-128 do the same for `FGComoduleCat.zero` via `.obj = ComoduleCat.zero`.
findings.fix: Make the construction opaque where possible and expose only characterization lemmas (`IsZero`, unique maps, and any needed `*_def` lemmas) rather than reducible bodies/carrier facts.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.zero` and `FGComoduleCat.zero` are public `abbrev`s, with `[simp]` lemmas exposing the carrier/coaction representation as `PUnit`; this leaks the implementation instead of forcing downstream code through the zero-object API.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "zero_hom_ext|hom_zero_ext|isZero_zero" TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Zero.lean` finds no such API; only `hasZeroObject` instances appear at lines 104 and 132.
findings.fix: Add characteristic lemmas for the named zero objects, e.g. `ComoduleCat.isZero_zero`, `FGComoduleCat.isZero_zero`, and `[simp]`/`@[ext]` lemmas that maps from or to these zeros are equal to `0`.
findings.issue: The named `FGComoduleCat.zero` has no direct `IsZero` theorem or morphism normal forms, so users of the roadmap-facing finite-dimensional category must unfold/full-subcategory internals or reason through the inherited existential zero object.

summary: The zero-object instance is present for both comodule categories, but the public API still exposes the concrete `PUnit` model while not providing the characteristic zero-object lemmas users should depend on.
